wrong_choice_explanations: を marks a direct object; います needs が for the subject that exists.
に marks a location, not the thing that exists.
で marks the place or means of an action, not the subject of existence.
を marks a direct object; rain is the subject of falling, not the object.
に marks a location or time, not the subject of a weather verb.
で marks the means or place; weather phenomena take が for the subject.
を marks a direct object, but 分かる specifically takes が for the thing understood.
に marks a target or time, not the object of understanding.
と means 'with' or 'and' and does not mark the object of 分かる.
を marks a direct object; the question asks about the subject who performed the action.
に marks a destination or time, not who performed the action.
は marks the topic, but question words like 誰 require が as the subject marker.
を marks a direct object, but 好き takes が for the liked thing.
に marks a target or location, not the object of preference.
と means 'with' or 'and' and cannot mark the thing that is liked.
を marks a direct object; the head is the subject experiencing pain.
に marks a location or time, not the body part that hurts.
で marks a place or means; body sensations use が for the affected part.
を marks a direct object; ある/ない takes が for the subject of existence.
に marks a location, not the thing that exists or doesn't.
から means 'from' and does not mark the subject of ありません.
を is used with the transitive 開ける, not the intransitive 開く; the window is the subject.
に marks a location, not the subject of the state.
で marks means or place of action, not the subject.
を marks a direct object, but できる specifically takes が for the ability complement.
に marks a target, not the thing one is able to do.
から means 'from' and does not mark the complement of できる.
を marks a direct object; flowers are the subject of blooming.
に marks a location or time, not the subject.
で marks the place or means, not the blooming subject.
